human:
SUMMARY:
please remove all changes from non Test and non Input
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces deprecated Java preview pattern guard syntax but inadvertently removes ALL pattern guard test coverage from the repo, since InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java is the only file testing pattern matching guards. The fix should update to Java 21 final syntax rather than replacing pattern guards with regular if statements.

POINTS:
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java] **CRITICAL: Pattern guard test regression.** This file is the ONLY file in the entire repo that tests Java pattern matching guards (`case ... && ...:` and `instanceof ... && ...`). The `LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck` listens on `TokenTypes.LAND`/`LOR`, which include the `&&` in pattern guards. By replacing all pattern guard syntax with regular `if` statements, this PR removes ALL test coverage for how the check handles pattern guards. The new `if`-based constructs duplicate coverage already present in `InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck.java`. Instead of removing pattern guards, UPDATE them to Java 21 final syntax: `case Integer i && i == 0:` (without parentheses around the pattern).
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java] **Misleading method names after change.** Methods named `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchStatement1`, `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueIfStatement1`, etc., reference "parenthesized" syntax that no longer exists after the PR. If pattern guards are reintroduced with correct Java 21 syntax, these names should be updated to remove "Parenthesized" (e.g., `typeGuardAfterTrueSwitchStatement1`).
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java] **Correct Java 21 instanceof pattern guard syntax.** The old preview syntax `o instanceof ((Integer i) && i == 0)` uses unnecessary parentheses. Java 21 final syntax is `o instanceof Integer i && i == 0`. If instanceof pattern guards are retained in the test, use this corrected form.
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheckTest.java] **Truncated diff — new coverage cases not visible.** The PR diff is truncated, so the new test cases added at lines 87, 89, 112 cannot be verified. Ensure these new cases exercise unique branches in `needOptimization()` that aren't already covered by the base test method `test()`.
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheckTest.java:96] Trailing blank line before closing brace — minor style inconsistency with the rest of the file.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces Java 22+ pattern guard syntax with simpler switch/if constructs to improve JaCoCo coverage, but introduces test message format inconsistency and loses pattern guard coverage entirely. The PR description is misleading — it claims to update to "Java 21 syntax" when pattern guards (JEP 441) are actually a Java 22+ preview feature being removed.

POINTS:
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheckTest.java:74-85] **Inconsistent message formatting**: `test()` uses `getCheckMessage(MSG_KEY, "&&", line, col)` while `testPatternDefinition()` uses hardcoded full message strings like `"41:49: Condition with && at line 41 position 48 need optimization..."`. Follow the existing convention from `test()` — use `getCheckMessage(MSG_KEY, "&&", 41, 48)` etc. — so that if the message template in `messages.properties` changes, only one place needs updating.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:5-20] **Regression: pattern guard coverage removed**: The original code tested pattern guards (`case (Integer i) && i == 0:`), which are JEP 441 (Java 22 preview, not "older Java" as the PR description claims). The new code replaces these with `case Integer i:` + nested `if` blocks, eliminating all pattern guard coverage. The `resources-noncompilable` directory exists precisely for testing newer Java syntax the parser can handle. Either restore pattern guard test cases or explicitly document this coverage drop.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:38-44] **New violations may be false positives for pattern matching**: The check flags `o instanceof Integer i && i == 0` because the left operand has `INSTANCEOF` and the right has no `METHOD_CALL`. However, with pattern matching, `i` is guaranteed non-null after the pattern match, so `i == 0` is safe regardless of ordering. The check (`LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck.java:85-102`) does not distinguish between traditional `instanceof` and pattern `instanceof` — consider whether these new violations represent meaningful defects or false positives that should be excluded when pattern variables are involved.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:26] **Redundant yield in switch expression**: `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression1` yields `"any"` in the `Integer i` arm and also has `default -> "any"`. This is logically redundant and confusing as a test input — if both branches yield the same value, consider simplifying or making them distinct to clarify intent.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces Java 21 pattern guard syntax with regular if-statements in the non-compilable test file, regressing coverage for instanceof pattern guards that the check actively processes. The PR description claims to "align with Java 21 syntax" but the diff shows the opposite — Java 21-specific pattern guard constructs are removed entirely.

POINTS:
- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/.../InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java] **REGRESSION: Pattern guard test coverage removed.** The original file tested Java 21 pattern guards (`case (Integer i) && i == 0:` and `o instanceof ((Integer i) && i == 0)`). The `LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck` implementation processes `LITERAL_INSTANCEOF` uniformly via `branchContains()` with no special exclusion for pattern guards, so these are valid and meaningful test cases. Replacing them with nested if-statements removes coverage for the check's behavior on pattern guard expressions.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/.../InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java] The PR description states the input "has been updated to align with Java 21 syntax" but the diff removes Java 21 pattern guard constructs entirely. If the original syntax was incorrect for Java 21, the PR description should explain why the original constructs were invalid and provide a reference to the correct Java 21 pattern guard syntax.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/.../InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:38-47] The old test expected violations at lines 32 and 66, corresponding to `o instanceof (((Integer i) && i == 2)) && (o = i) != null`. The assignment `(o = i)` appearing before a comparison in a `&&` chain was the violation trigger. The new test preserves this semantic pattern (e.g., line 43: `o instanceof Integer i && i == 2 && (o = i) != null`) but no longer exercises it within a pattern guard context. Consider whether pattern guard coverage should be retained alongside the new instanceof pattern-matching cases.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/resources-noncompilable/.../InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java:5-32] The refactored methods `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchStatement1`, `typeGuardAfterParenthesizedTrueSwitchExpression1`, and their counterparts no longer contain any pattern guard logic — they now use nested if-statements inside switch cases. The method names are misleading since they no longer test "type guard after parenthesized true" patterns. Consider renaming these methods to reflect their actual content.

- [sevntu-checks/src/test/java/.../LogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheckTest.java:74-87] The new expected violations reference lines 41, 43, 87, 89, 112 in the restructured input file. Given the significant restructuring of `InputLogicConditionNeedOptimizationCheck2.java` (from ~80 lines to ~120+ lines), verify these line numbers are accurate. A single line change elsewhere could shift them and cause silent test misalignment.
